# Test handling of format specifiers
cat > format_schema.json << 'EOF'
{
  "type": "object",
  "description": "Schema with various format specifiers",
  "properties": {
    "id": {
      "type": "string",
      "format": "uuid",
      "description": "Unique identifier"
    },
    "created": {
      "type": "string",
      "format": "date-time",
      "description": "Creation timestamp"
    },
    "updated": {
      "type": "string",
      "format": "date-time",
      "description": "Last update timestamp"
    },
    "email": {
      "type": "string",
      "format": "email",
      "description": "Email address"
    },
    "url": {
      "type": "string",
      "format": "uri",
      "description": "Website URL"
    },
    "data": {
      "type": "string",
      "format": "byte",
      "description": "Binary data"
    }
  }
}
EOF

# Process with formats
exec mcptrace2gostruct -package formats format_schema.json
stdout 'package formats'
# Check that time.Time is used for date-time format
stdout 'Created time.Time'
stdout 'Updated time.Time'
stdout 'import \(\n\t"encoding/json"\n\t"time"\n\)'
# Check that []byte is used for byte format
stdout 'Data \[\]byte'
! stderr 'ERROR'
